edibility: Premier edible bolete; eaten fresh, dried, in risotto/pasta/sauces; one of world's most prized mushrooms for culinary use.
taste: Nutty, meaty, intense umami flavor; firm flesh with excellent texture when cooked.
search: Found late summer through fall in mycorrhizal relationships with conifers and hardwoods; lacks a veil and ring, with white pores that age to yellowish. Key look-alikes include Boletus erythropus (edible but lower quality) and bitter Tylopilus felleus (toxic, easily identified by pink spore print).
